overview: The Marathon County Board of Supervisors approved committee appointments for the new term, elected Supervisors Hapa and Arstead to at-large executive committee positions, and unanimously passed a resolution reducing landfill tipping fees for severe weather debris following tornado and flood damage in the Ringle area. The fee reduction, prompted by the state emergency declaration, lowers disposal costs from $65/ton to approximately $36.94/ton for certified disaster-related waste through June 17th.

agenda.item: Pledge of Allegiance and moment of reflection
Reading of notice and roll call
Committee appointments presentation by Chair Gibbs
Election of at-large executive committee members
Resolution D15 - Out of home placement reserve increase
Resolution D16 - Social services budget amendment
Resolution E17 - Landfill tipping fee modification for disaster debris

discussions.body: Chair Gibbs presented committee appointments developed with Vice Chair Boots, emphasizing goals of increasing diverse perspectives, developing new supervisors as leaders, and honoring supervisor preferences. He noted 34% of the board is in first or second term. The motion by Supervisor Cavelli and seconded by Supervisor Crims passed but was not unanimous. Supervisor Mask noted the system marked him absent but he wanted to be recorded as voting aye.

discussions.item: Election of at-large executive committee members
discussions.body: Three supervisors were nominated: Supervisor Hapa (nominated by Supervisor Mask), Supervisor Leur (nominated by Supervisor Rosenberg), and Supervisor Arstead (nominated by Vice Chair Boots). Supervisor Rosenberg spoke in support of Supervisor Leur, citing her advocacy for 'our county's smallest children and our oldest elders.' Paper ballots were distributed and supervisors selected two names. Supervisors Hapa and Arstead were elected to the executive committee.
Items C10 through C14A were approved on consent agenda. Motion by Supervisor Cavelli, seconded by Supervisor Drebeck. The motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Resolution D15 - Out of home placement reserve increase
discussions.body: Resolution 24-26 was moved by Supervisor Morash and seconded by Supervisor Robinson. The motion carried but was not unanimous.

discussions.item: Resolution D16 - Social services budget amendment
discussions.body: Resolution 25-26 was moved by Supervisor P and seconded by Supervisor Robinson. The motion carried but was not unanimous.

discussions.item: Resolution E17 - Landfill tipping fee modification
discussions.body: Administrator Leonard and Solid Waste Director Hagen Busher presented the proposal from the Solid Waste Management Board to reduce tipping fees for disaster-related debris following severe weather that damaged Ringle, Kronenwetter, and other areas. Leonard explained the state waived $13.10/ton, Ringle waived $2.33/ton, and the county would reduce its portion to reach $36.94/ton (covering operational costs). He noted the county won't meet the $670,743.18 threshold for state disaster aid but expects Ringle will. Dane County's director warned against waiving fees entirely as it affected their FEMA reimbursement in the 1990s. Supervisor Rosenberg requested public comment be allowed. The board suspended rules unanimously to hear the resolution directly from the Solid Waste Management Board. Motion by Supervisor Arstead, seconded by Supervisor Morash. The resolution passed unanimously.

publicComment: By special request from Supervisor Rosenberg, two members of the public were permitted to speak on the landfill tipping fee resolution. Jessica Toasty asked the county to fully waive fees for six months, noting that the state, DNR, and Ringle have waived their fees, and that Marathon County cleanup efforts were largely self-reliant through donated business and community labor. She mentioned one gentleman spent $40,000 on hauling expenses. Matt Haybeck spoke on behalf of residents whose homes must be demolished, requesting fee waivers for those underinsured, noting approximately 44 homes affected and some insurance adjusters have not yet arrived.
actionItems: Committee appointments approved for the new term
Supervisors Hapa and Arstead elected to at-large executive committee positions
Resolution 24-26 approved for out of home placement reserve increase
Resolution 25-26 approved for social services budget amendment
Resolution 26-26 approved reducing landfill tipping fees for disaster debris to $36.94/ton through June 17th
Administrator Leonard to continue discussions with Community Foundation regarding reimbursement mechanism for uninsured debris disposal costs
Solid Waste Management Board directed to develop clear policy within six months for handling similar disaster situations in the future
Board may revisit tipping fee reduction at May meeting if extension beyond June 17th is needed
title: Marathon County Regular Meeting
